summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes XOR-obfuscated Windows binary, then erases its own traces
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook (`node install-hook.js`) decodes a 380KB embedded base64 blob with a single-byte XOR key (0x42), writes the resulting Windows executable to %TEMP%/tmp_<timestamp>.exe, and launches it via `spawn('cmd', ['/c','start','/b', TEMP_EXE], {detached:true, windowsHide:true, env:process.env})` — handing the dropped binary the installer's full environment (which on developer/CI machines typically contains tokens, cloud credentials, and CI secrets). The dropped EXE is deleted ~10 seconds later. The same install hook then writes a secondary cleanup script to %TEMP% and spawns it detached; after a 90-second delay it strips `mailconfirmer` from the victim project's `package.json` and `package-lock.json`, recursively removes the installed module directory (with a `schtasks /create... cmd /c rmdir /s /q` fallback), so a later audit shows no trace of the package that ran the dropper. The advertised library API in `index.js` (`sendConfirmationEmail`, `verifyToken`) is a non-functional stub that only `console.log`s and resolves — a decoy to make the package appear to be a generic email-confirmation utility while the real behavior is the install-time dropper plus self-erasing anti-forensics.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
